name: RETREAT_GUARD_V1 (SPECIFICATION ONLY - not implemented this task, per section 6)
problem: Audit finding I (upheld): only _retreat_pivot (Cinderace->ready Mega) exists; there is no general anti-retreat veto, so search may retreat Mega Starmie needlessly or retreat without a ready replacement.
scope: A veto layer in _veto_search_pick (search-sourced RETREAT picks only); heuristic retreat (_retreat_pivot) is unchanged.
veto_a_search_retreat_when: the active is Mega Starmie ex AND there is NO benched Pokemon ready to attack next turn (no benched Mega Starmie with energy / no benched attacker), OR
retreating strands us (the replacement cannot attack and is not safer), OR
the retreat moves us INTO an immediate KO of the promoted Pokemon, OR
the only reason is that the active cannot attack THIS turn (chip/develop is better than burning the retreat energy).
allow_retreat_when: a benched attacker is ready (energy + a usable attack) and is a strictly better attacker, OR
retreating escapes a lock/special-condition (asleep/paralyzed/effect) that prevents attacking, OR
survival gain (avoid a lethal next turn) clearly exceeds the [C][C] energy cost.
replacement_action_when_vetoed: the best attack (chip) if available, else END; never a worse retreat.
validation_required_before_implementing: a real-observation survey of when choose_action returns RETREAT (count, board states, whether a ready replacement existed) + an A/B vs baseline; do NOT bundle with the develop rule.
status: SPEC ONLY - implement in a later isolated task if the develop rule is rejected or after it lands.
